_fixture_meta.purpose: Placeholder for a real Vapi tool-calls webhook payload.
replace: message.call.assistantId → real VAPI assistantId (restaurant or booking track)
message.toolCallList[0].id → real tool-call ID from Vapi (format: 'call_xxx...')
message.toolCallList[0].function.name → real tool name (e.g. get_menu, create_callback_request)
message.toolCallList[0].function.arguments → real arguments object or JSON string
factory_delta: call.orgId            — NOT present in factory buildVapiToolCall
toolWithToolCallList  — NOT present in factory; real Vapi includes this alongside toolCallList
function.arguments as string — factory always sends object; real Vapi may send JSON string
toolCallList[].type   — factory uses 'function'; real payloads confirm this but with extra metadata
_fixture_meta.arguments_warning: In real Vapi payloads, function.arguments can be a JSON-encoded string instead of an object. If your backend deserializes arguments, test both forms.

function.name: get_menu
function.description: Fetches the restaurant menu with categories and items.
